item.title: Berna approves burial with pets: new rules for 2026
item.description: From 2026, in Berna it will be possible to be buried with your pet in dedicated areas. Here's what changes and how it works
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New cemetery regulation approved in Bern
- Burial with pets in dedicated areas
- Pets interred in urns

## Key facts
- **What**: New cemetery regulation
- When: April 24, 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: Bern municipal council
- Amount: Not specified

The Bern municipal council approved on Thursday the new cemetery regulation that allows being buried with one's pet. Dedicated areas, called 'Person with pet', will be created specifically for this new burial method. Pets must be interred in urns. This decision is part of a complete revision of funeral regulations that modernizes cemetery provisions.

Among other novelties, people will be able to be buried in sections dedicated to minors up to the age of 18, whereas the previous limit was 14 years. All the planned changes have received cross-party consensus, except for a proposal by the faction composed of Alternative Left, Labour Party, and Alternative Forum, which asked to ban the deliberate killing of animals to be buried with a person. The Council deemed that the cemetery regulation was not the appropriate venue to enact such a provision.

The Municipality was outvoted regarding competencies: in the future, it will be the Municipal Council that decides on major renovations and the suppression of cemeteries, a prerogative that the executive would have preferred to maintain. The Municipal Council unanimously approved the completely revised regulation, waiving a second examination of the matter.

### Complete revision of funeral regulations

The new regulation stems from a postulate by the Green Liberals that the Municipal Council had accepted in March 2021. During the project's development, it emerged that cemetery legislation no longer corresponded to current reality and practices, partly based on obsolete legal foundations. A complete revision was therefore opted for. The needs of society have profoundly changed, stated the city executive. In 2024, the share of traditional burials was only 16.3%. Other forms of burial are increasingly requested.

'During the examination of the matter, many issues emerged that the revision has now clarified,' declared in the chamber the municipal responsible Matthias Aebischer (PS). Often, discussions took on a philosophical character. 'The cemeteries of the city of Bern represent a model at the Swiss level,' he stated. 'I am proud of this.'

### Cemeteries appreciated as parks

The city of Bern manages three cemeteries: Bremgarten, Schosshalden, and Bümpliz. According to its website, it administers a total of 13,000 graves. The cemeteries are also appreciated as public green areas for nearby recreation. In 1999, the burial field for prematurely born children and the area for Muslim graves were created. In 2018, the city inaugurated the Buddhist burial field, and in 2025, the Alevite one will be opened at the Bremgarten cemetery. - ## Practical implications for cross-border workers

The new regulation on burial with pets in Bern could have practical implications for cross-border workers living or working in Ticino. For example, those who own a pet and wish to be buried with it will need to consider the new provisions. Areas dedicated to 'Person with animal' will be created specifically for this type of burial, which could influence future decisions regarding burial locations.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the revision of the cemetery regulation, it was not possible to be buried with one's pet. The new regulation represents a significant change, in line with the modern needs of society. The possibility of being buried with one's pet reflects a growing interest in animal welfare and their integration into daily life.

### Concrete scenarios

Suppose a cross-border worker living in Ticino and working in Bern wishes to be buried with their dog. With the new regulation, it will be possible to choose one of the dedicated 'Person with animal' areas in Bern's cemeteries. However, it will be necessary to inter the animal in an urn, which could involve additional costs and specific procedures.

### Concrete procedures

To take advantage of the new provision, citizens will need to follow some concrete procedures. First, it will be necessary to contact Bern's cemetery office for detailed information on the dedicated areas and associated costs. Subsequently, it will be possible to book a specific area for burial with one's pet. It will also be important to consider the provisions for interring the animal in an urn.

### Comparison with other forms of burial

The new regulation represents just one of the many forms of burial available in Bern. In addition to traditional burials, the city also offers burial fields for prematurely born girls and boys, areas for Muslim, Buddhist, and Alevite graves. The diversity of options reflects the growing demand for personalized burial methods respectful of different cultural and religious traditions.

### Impact on funeral planning

The possibility of being buried with one's pet could influence the funeral planning of many citizens. Those who own a pet may want to consider this option as part of their future plans. It will be important to inform themselves promptly about the procedures and associated costs to ensure that all arrangements are in order.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to benefit from the new regulation

To benefit from the new regulation on burial with pets in Bern, it is important to follow some concrete steps. First, contact the Bern cemetery office to obtain detailed information about the dedicated areas and associated costs. Next, book a specific area for burial with your pet. Make sure to follow all procedures for the interment of the animal in an urn.

### Concrete steps

1. Contact the Bern cemetery office for detailed information.
2. Book a specific area for burial with your pet.
3. Follow all procedures for the interment of the animal in an urn.
4. Verify the associated costs and ensure that all arrangements are in order.

### Useful tools

For further information and to plan the burial with your pet, you can use the [funeral cost calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool can help estimate the costs associated with the burial and plan the expenses efficiently.

### Conclusion

The new regulation on burial with pets in Bern represents a significant step towards the modernization of funeral regulations. For cross-border workers living or working in Ticino, this new option could influence future decisions regarding burial sites. It is important to inform yourself promptly and follow the necessary procedures to ensure that all arrangements are in order. Using tools like the [funeral cost calculator](nav:calculator) can help plan expenses efficiently and make informed decisions.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/berna-d%c3%a0-il-via-libera-alla-sepoltura-con-il-proprio-animale/91309382)*

item.title: Domestic violence: Zurich model proves convincing
item.description: The pilot project for real-time electronic monitoring to combat domestic violence has yielded positive results. The goal is to extend it nationwide by 2027.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Pilot project Zurich: real-time electronic monitoring
- Low costs: around 60,000 francs
- Goal: national extension by 2027

## Key facts
- **What**: Pilot project of real-time electronic monitoring to combat domestic violence
- When: Project launched on April 30, 2021
- Where: Canton of Zurich
- Who: Intercantonal association led by Vassilis Venizelos
- Amount: Total cost of around 60,000 francs

Vassilis Venizelos, president of the intercantonal association, presented to RSI the results of the pilot project of real-time electronic monitoring to combat domestic violence. The project, carried out in the Canton of Zurich, demonstrated the effectiveness of active and dynamic monitoring at low costs. The total expenditure was around 60,000 francs, also thanks to the use of a private monitoring center. The project highlighted the importance of developing such measures on a national or, at least, intercantonal scale, as domestic violence does not recognize borders. A potential perpetrator can move from one canton to another, and for this reason, coordinated tools and common procedures are needed.

In Zurich, about ten cases were monitored. It is difficult to determine how many episodes were actually prevented, but the project allowed to concretely evaluate the police's response capacity. A decisive element was the definition of the prohibition perimeters: in the first phase, a radius of one kilometer, the so-called Zurich model, was tested. The experience has now been made available to other cantons in the form of a real operational kit, with indications, instructions, and procedures already tested.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The pilot project of real-time electronic monitoring to combat domestic violence has significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Active and dynamic monitoring, at low costs, represents a step forward in the fight against domestic violence, a phenomenon that does not recognize borders. Cross-border workers can benefit from greater security and protection, thanks to the collaboration between the cantons and the use of coordinated tools and common procedures. The goal of creating a single national monitoring center by 2027 represents a further step towards greater coherence in the treatment of domestic violence cases, ensuring more effective protection for all victims, regardless of the canton in which they are located. ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The pilot project for real-time electronic monitoring to combat domestic violence has significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Active and dynamic surveillance, with contained costs, represents a step forward in the fight against domestic violence, a phenomenon that knows no borders. Cross-border workers can benefit from greater security and protection, thanks to the collaboration between the Cantons and the use of coordinated tools and common procedures.

The goal of creating a single national surveillance center by 2027 represents a further step towards greater consistency in the treatment of domestic violence cases. This means that victims of domestic violence, regardless of the Canton in which they are located, will be able to benefit from more effective and coordinated protection. Specific training for police officers on the technical aspects of the device, operational procedures, and information management is another key element to ensure the effectiveness of the system.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the introduction of the pilot project for real-time electronic monitoring, the fight against domestic violence in Switzerland was characterized by a fragmentation of procedures and protective measures. Each Canton had its own regulations and methods of intervention, which made it difficult to guarantee uniform and coordinated protection for victims of domestic violence. With the introduction of the Zurich model and the aim of extending it nationally, a significant improvement in the management of domestic violence cases is expected, with greater effectiveness and consistency in protective measures.

### Concrete Scenarios

A concrete example of how the pilot project can make a difference is represented by the definition of prohibition perimeters. In the first phase of the project, a radius of one kilometer, the so-called Zurich model, was tested. This allowed for the concrete evaluation of the police's response capacity and the definition of effective operational procedures. With the extension of the model at the national level, it is expected that victims of domestic violence will be able to benefit from more effective and timely protection, regardless of the Canton in which they are located.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Action: what to do concretely

For cross-border workers who wish to delve into the protective measures against domestic violence, it is important to stay updated on the latest news and operational procedures. The pilot project of real-time electronic surveillance represents a significant step forward in the fight against domestic violence, and the goal of extending it nationwide by 2027 offers new opportunities for protection and security.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. **Stay informed**: Keep up-to-date with the latest news regarding the pilot project of real-time electronic surveillance. Consult the official websites and communications from the cantonal authorities.
2. Contact the authorities: If necessary, contact the local authorities to obtain detailed information on the available protective measures and operational procedures.
3. Participate in training: If you are a police officer, participate in the specific training on the technical aspects of the device, operational procedures, and information management.
4. Use the available tools: Take advantage of the tools made available by other cantons, such as the operational kit with instructions, guidelines, and procedures already tested.
5. Collaborate with private alarm centers: If necessary, collaborate with private alarm centers to ensure active and dynamic surveillance.

### Site tools

For further information and to delve into the protective measures against domestic violence, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare salaries and working conditions in Ticino. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the site to stay updated on the latest news and operational procedures.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3697581)*

item.title: EOC Midwives: Conference on Physiological Birth in Mendrisio
item.description: On May 6, 2026, EOC is organizing a public conference on midwifery care at Mendrisio Hospital. Focus on physiological birth and continuity of care.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Midwifery conference EOC on May 6, 2026 in Mendrisio
- Focus on physiological birth and continuity of care
- Entertainment area for children aged 4-10

## Key facts
- **What**: Public conference on midwifery care
- When: May 6, 2026, 16:30-19:00
- Where: Ospedale Beata Vergine, Mendrisio
- Who: Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC)
- Cost: Not specified

On May 6, 2026, the Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) will organize a public conference on midwifery care at the Ospedale Beata Vergine in Mendrisio. The event, on the occasion of International Midwife Day, will take place from 16:30 to 19:00 in the auditorium located on the -1 floor. The initiative aims to promote and enhance midwifery care throughout the birth process, with a particular focus on physiological birth.

During the conference, the team of specialists from the Women and Children's Department will present models of care that promote physiological birth. The event, moderated by PD Dr. med. Andrea Braga, is aimed at the public and all professional figures involved in the birth process. The goal is to foster a constructive exchange on best practices in midwifery care.

To allow parents with children to participate, from 17:00 to 19:00 there will be a supervised entertainment area for children aged 4 to 10. The young guests will also be offered a healthy snack.

### Detailed program

The conference program includes several thematic sessions:

- 16:30: Welcome
- 17:00: Welcome by PD Dr. med. Andrea Braga and Isabella Guarisco
- 17:10: The midwife and her office; perspectives and benefits for women, with Esther Voce Heine, Valentina Magni, Giada Rampini
- 17:30: Discussion
- 17:40: The perineum among the protagonists of childbirth; indications, advantages, clinical experience and best practices, with Margaretha Luethi and Alessandra Turcatti
- 18:00: Discussion
- 18:10: Promoting physiological birth; the role of the midwife, with Laura Loiodice and Silvia Holzknecht
- 18:30: Discussion
- 18:40: The 1000 days of birth; postpartum continuity of care, early and informed support, with Gilda Gobbi and Stephanie Todaro
- 19:00: Discussion
- 19:10: Refreshments

The children's entertainment area will be available from 17:00 to 19:00, only by registration via email to comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch. The conference organized by EOC represents a unique opportunity to delve into the crucial role of midwives in the birth process. Physiological birth, which refers to natural childbirth without medical interventions, is at the center of attention. This approach aims to minimize unnecessary medical interventions, promoting a more natural and respectful birth experience for both maternal and fetal physiology.

The exchange between professionals and the public is fundamental to continuously improve obstetric practices. The discussions planned during the conference will allow for sharing experiences and knowledge, contributing to a constant improvement in the quality of obstetric care.

### The importance of continuity of care

Another central theme of the conference is postpartum continuity of care. The first 1000 days of a child's life are crucial for their development and well-being. Postpartum continuity of care ensures that the mother and child receive the necessary support during this delicate period. This includes emotional support, health education, and monitoring of both the mother's and child's health.

### Practical implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, the conference represents an opportunity to better understand the Swiss healthcare system and local obstetric practices. Participation in such events can help reduce linguistic and cultural barriers, facilitating access to quality healthcare services.

Furthermore, the conference offers the opportunity to learn about the resources available for families, such as children's play areas during public events. This can be particularly useful for cross-border workers seeking to balance work and family responsibilities.

### Conclusion

The EOC's conference on obstetric care in Mendrisio is a significant event for the community. It offers a platform for dialogue and continuous improvement of obstetric practices, while promoting the health and well-being of mothers and children. For cross-border workers, it represents an opportunity to better integrate into the Swiss healthcare system and access useful resources for their families.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). To participate in the obstetric care conference organized by EOC in Mendrisio, you need to follow a few simple steps. First, make sure to register your children for the entertainment area, if necessary. You can do this by sending an email to comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch. Remember to provide all the required information to ensure a safe and pleasant experience for your little ones.

### Registration Procedure

1. **Send an email**: Contact comunicazione.mendrisio@eoc.ch to register your children for the entertainment area.
2. Provide the necessary information: Make sure to include your children's names and ages, as well as any other required information.
3. Confirm your participation: You will receive a confirmation of your registration and all the necessary information for the day of the event.

### What to Bring With You

- Identification documents: Bring a valid ID for yourself and your children.
- Contact information: Make sure you have your phone number and email address handy.
- Comfortable clothing: Wear comfortable clothes for you and your children, so you can actively participate in the sessions and enjoy the entertainment area.

### Use Our Healthcare Cost Calculator

To best plan your participation in the conference, you can use our [healthcare cost calculator](nav:health). This tool will help you estimate the costs associated with obstetric care and other healthcare expenses, ensuring you are prepared for any eventuality.

### Conclusion

The obstetric care conference organized by EOC in Mendrisio is a must-attend event for anyone interested in maternal and child well-being. Follow the steps above to ensure you participate and make the most of all the available resources. Don't forget to use our healthcare cost calculator to best plan your participation and ensure a positive experience for you and your family.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921155/la-nascita-e-il-ruolo-dell-ostetrica-accanto-alla-donna-ne-parla-l-eoc)*

item.title: Yellow alert for thunderstorms across the province of Varese
item.description: The Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region has issued a yellow weather alert for thunderstorms across the entire province of Varese, with risks of damage and hydraulic criticalities.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Yellow alert for thunderstorms in Varese
- Risks of damage to roofs and structures
- Possible localized hydrogeological instability

## Key facts
- **What**: Yellow weather alert for thunderstorms
- When: April 27, 2026, from 12:00
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region
- Amount: Not specified

The Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region issued a weather alert today, Monday, April 27, 2026, signaling a yellow-level thunderstorm risk in part of the province of Varese. The Varesotto territory is divided into two homogeneous alert zones with different scenarios. The Lakes and Varesine Prealps area is subject to the yellow alert for thunderstorms already from 12 noon today, expiring at 14:00 tomorrow, Tuesday, April 28. The area of the lower province, which also includes part of the province of Varese together with Milan, Monza Brianza, Como, and Lecco, is instead subject to a yellow alert for thunderstorms starting from midnight tonight until 14:00 tomorrow.

For both areas, the hydrogeological, hydraulic, and strong wind risks remain at the green level for the moment. On the meteorological level, in the afternoon and evening of today, scattered showers or thunderstorms are possible in the northern sectors, even of moderate intensity, with possible hail of small and medium size. During the day tomorrow, the widespread precipitation, expected during the night and in the morning, is forecast to gradually decrease in the afternoon.

### Risks and precautions

Civil Protection asks local systems to maintain an operational phase of Attention, preparing for the prompt activation of monitoring actions. Among the risks reported: damage to roofs and structures, falling trees, problems with traffic and overhead service networks, possible localized hydrogeological instability, and critical issues in the hydraulic network and urban drainage systems.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in the province of Varese, it is important to be aware of the risks related to thunderstorms. In case of bad weather, it is advisable to avoid unnecessary movements and constantly monitor weather forecasts. Moreover, it is fundamental to ensure that one's homes and vehicles are prepared for possible adverse weather conditions.

### Practical advice
- Constant monitoring: Use weather forecast apps and websites for real-time updates.
- Preparation of homes: Ensure that roofs, windows, and doors are well closed and that there are no objects that could be carried away by the wind.
- Traffic: Avoid driving during thunderstorms, especially in areas prone to flooding or landslides.
- Emergencies: Have an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, and essential medications at hand. ## Analysis of Critical Issues

The yellow alert issued by the Civil Protection of the Lombardy Region for the province of Varese represents an important signal for the population and, in particular, for cross-border workers who daily move between Ticino and Lombardy. The forecasted thunderstorms can have a significant impact on traffic and the safety of infrastructure, making specific precautions necessary.

### Impact on Traffic

One of the main risks reported is represented by traffic issues. Intense thunderstorms can cause flooding, landslides, and falling trees, making roads and highways dangerous. For cross-border workers who daily use the connecting roads between Ticino and the province of Varese, it is fundamental to plan movements in advance and consider possible alternatives in case of road closures or slowdowns.

### Hydraulic and Hydrogeological Criticalities

Intense precipitation can also cause hydraulic and hydrogeological criticalities, such as floods and landslides. Urban drainage networks might not be sufficient to manage the volume of water, increasing the risk of flooding in urban areas. It is therefore important that residents and cross-border workers verify the conditions of their homes and be ready to intervene in case of emergency.

### Preparation of Homes

To minimize the damage caused by thunderstorms, it is advisable to adopt some preventive measures. For example, ensure that gutters are clean and functional, that windows and doors are well sealed, and that there are no objects outside that could be carried away by the wind. Additionally, it is useful to have an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, and essential medications.

### Constant Monitoring

Another crucial aspect is the constant monitoring of weather forecasts. Using reliable weather forecast apps and websites can provide real-time updates on weather conditions and allow for informed decisions. In case of a weather alert, it is important to follow the indications of local authorities and stay in contact with emergency services.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino, it is fundamental to be prepared for possible disruptions in traffic or closures of border crossings. Planning movements in advance and considering possible alternatives can help avoid delays and ensure safety during the journey. Additionally, it is important to be aware of the risks related to thunderstorms and adopt the necessary measures to protect homes and vehicles. ## What to do in case of weather alert

In case of a yellow weather alert for thunderstorms, it is important to follow some guidelines to ensure your safety and that of your family. Here is a series of steps to follow:

1. **Monitor weather forecasts**: Use reliable weather forecast apps and websites for real-time updates on weather conditions.
2. Plan your trips: Avoid unnecessary travel and, if possible, postpone trips until weather conditions improve.
3. Prepare your home: Ensure that roofs, windows, and doors are well closed and that there are no objects outside that could be carried away by the wind.
4. Have an emergency kit: Prepare an emergency kit with water, food, flashlight, essential medications, and other necessities.
5. Follow the authorities' instructions: In case of a weather alert, follow the instructions of local authorities and stay in contact with emergency services.
6. Check road conditions: Before setting off, check road conditions and consider alternatives in case of closures or delays.
7. Protect your vehicles: Ensure that vehicles are parked in safe places and are not exposed to risks of flooding or falling trees.
8. Inform your family: In case of travel, inform your family of your itinerary and expected arrival.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to constantly monitor weather conditions and plan their trips, tools such as the [weather conditions calculator](nav:calculator) and the [weather forecast comparator](nav:exchange) can be useful. These tools can provide detailed information on weather conditions and help plan trips safely and efficiently.

### Conclusion

The yellow weather alert for thunderstorms across the province of Varese is an important signal for the population, and in particular for cross-border workers who daily travel between Ticino and Lombardy. By following the guidelines and adopting the necessary preventive measures, it is possible to minimize risks and ensure your safety during thunderstorms. Using tools such as the [weather conditions calculator](nav:calculator) and the [weather forecast comparator](nav:exchange) can help plan trips safely and efficiently.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/allerta-gialla-per-temporali-su-tutta-la-provincia-di-varese/2559307/)*
